title: L3: Binary Digits
description: Explaining bits as binary digits and measuring the length of bit sequences.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from Lesson 2. Count carefully and use the options given.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-dna',questions:[{"prompt":"How many symbols are in ...--- ?","answer":"6","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which uses dots and dashes?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many binary symbols are in 1010?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = light flashes, B = coding rules. Which is a physical medium?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Bits
steps.content:
Binary digits

A bit is one binary digit. Binary means there are only two possible symbols: 0 and 1. A bit is not the value 0 and 1 at the same time. It is one choice: either 0 or 1.

QuestionAnswer
What symbols can a bit use?0 or 1
How many symbols is one bit?1 symbol
How do you find the length of a bit sequence?Count the 0s and 1s

A sequence such as 10110 has five bits because it contains five binary digits.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-intro-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A bit can use A = 0 and 1, B = 0 to 9, C = any letter. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: binary uses 0 and 1."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 10110?","answer":"5","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Key Ideas: Bits and Sequences
steps.content:
Key ideas

Binary uses only two symbols: 0 and 1. A bit is one binary digit. A bit sequence is several bits placed in order.

ExampleWhat to notice
0One bit
1One bit
10Two bits
10110Five bits
102Not binary because it contains 2

A leading zero still counts as a bit. For example, 0011 has four bits.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-vocab-check',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in 0?","answer":"1","right":"Correct: one symbol is one bit."},{"prompt":"Is 102 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct: it contains 2."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Count Bits
steps.content:
Step through a bit sequence

Click through how to describe and measure a sequence of bits.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr3-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"Look at the sequence","html":"
10110
","note":"Every symbol must be either 0 or 1 for the sequence to be binary."},{"title":"Check the symbols","html":"
PositionSymbol
11
20
31
41
50
","note":"All five symbols are binary digits."},{"title":"Measure its length","html":"

10110 has 5 bits

","note":"The length of a bit sequence is the number of binary digits it contains."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"In 10110, how many symbols are shown?","answer":"5","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is every symbol in 10110 either 0 or 1? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Is It Binary?
steps.content:
Step through valid and invalid sequences

Click through how to decide whether a sequence is binary.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr3-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"Check 10101","html":"

1 0 1 0 1

","note":"Every symbol is 0 or 1, so this is binary."},{"title":"Count 10101","html":"

5 symbols = 5 bits

","note":"Count every binary digit."},{"title":"Check 1201","html":"

1 2 0 1

","note":"This is not binary because it contains 2."},{"title":"Check 0000","html":"

0 0 0 0

","note":"This is binary. Zeros are valid binary digits and each zero still counts."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Is 10101 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 1201 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct: it contains 2."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: One Skill at a Time
steps.content:
Mini check

First decide whether the sequence is binary. Then count the bits only when it is binary.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-mini-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Is 1010 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1010?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 1012 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct: it contains 2."},{"prompt":"Is 0001 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 0001?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: count bits

Type the number of bits in each sequence.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-practice-a',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1?","answer":"1","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 10?","answer":"2","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 101?","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 00001111?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 110010?","answer":"6","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: binary or not

A binary sequence can only use 0 and 1. Type yes or no.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-practice-b',questions:[{"prompt":"Is 101010 a binary sequence?","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct. It only uses 0 and 1."},{"prompt":"Is 10201 a binary sequence?","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct. It contains 2."},{"prompt":"Is 000000 a binary sequence?","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct. 0 is allowed."},{"prompt":"Is 11111 a binary sequence?","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct. 1 is allowed."},{"prompt":"Is 10A01 a binary sequence?","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct. It contains A."}]});

steps.title: Extension: Longer Sequences
steps.content:
Extension challenge

Fast finishers can try longer sequences. The method is the same: check the symbols, then count them.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-extension',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1010101010?","answer":"10","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 11110000 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 11110000?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 100A010 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct: it contains A."}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated bit questions correctly to complete this step.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1111?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 10000001?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A bit can use A = 0 and 1, B = 0 to 9, C = any letter. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 10012 a valid binary sequence? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 010101 valid binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["yes","y"],"right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer all questions.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr3-exam',questions:[{"prompt":"A bit is one binary what?","answers":["digit","binary digit"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 10110110?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A bit can use A = 0 and 1, B = 1 and 2, C = A and B. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 2010 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 0000?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."}]});

quizQuestions.q: What two symbols can a bit use?
Which pair matches a binary idea?
Why is 10021 not a binary sequence?
The length of a bit sequence is...
options: the number of binary digits it contains
the number of letters in the word binary
